name: Corn Souffle
keywords: corn eggs main dish mexican sauces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Butter a 1-quart souffle dish or casserole.
3. Melt margarine in a 2-quart saucepan over low heat.
4. Stir in flour, sugar, cumin, nutmeg, and red pepper until smooth.
5. Cook mixture on low heat, stirring constantly, until bubbly.
6. Add milk, heat to boiling, stirring constantly.
7. Boil for 1 minute after reaching a boil.
8. Beat egg yolks slightly in a medium bowl.
9. Gradually stir at least half of the hot mixture into the egg yolks.
10. Stir the hot mixture back into the saucepan and bring to a simmer.
11. Remove from heat and stir in onion, green chiles, and corn.
12. Beat egg whites in another medium bowl on high speed until stiff.
13. Fold about 1/4 of the egg whites into the corn mixture.
14. Carefully pour the remaining batter into the prepared souffle dish.
15. Bake uncovered until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean (about 50 minutes).
16. Prepare Green Chile Cheese Sauce and serve with the souffle when done.
Green Chile Cheese Sauce:
1. Heat all ingredients over low heat, stirring constantly, until cheese is melted.
